reasoning:
The forecasting relies entirely on climatological averages for Paris (Orly) as we have no real-time weather feed for these 2025 dates. The daily mean temperature follows a seasonal decline from early November through February. Historically, the daily mean around Nov 9 is ~8.5-9.0°C. For Nov 16, the mean drops roughly 1.0°C to ~7.5-8.0°C. For Dec 9, it drops significantly to ~5.0-5.5°C. For early Feb, it remains around 5.0°C but with slightly higher variance. 

Letting T0 be the temperature on Nov 9, and T1, T2, T3 be the temperatures on the resolution dates, we calculate the probability that Ti > T0 assuming normally distributed daily temperatures with mean differences driven by seasonal averages and a pooled variance. T1 - T0 has a mean of ~-1.0°C, leading to a probability of ~42-44%. T2 - T0 has a mean difference of ~-3.0°C, lowering the probability to ~28-30%. T3 - T0 has a mean difference of ~-3.5°C; despite slightly higher variance in late winter, the expectation is still markedly lower than November, yielding ~27-29%. Seasonal climatic patterns strictly dictate that the December and February dates are noticeably colder than early November on average, pushing these probabilities firmly below 0.5.
